heading: Prohibitions against certain unfair and deceptive Internet sales practices
label: 8402 Prohibitions against certain unfair and deceptive Internet sales practices

children.heading: Requirements for certain Internet-based sales
children.label: a Requirements for certain Internet-based sales
A
children.text: a description of the goods or services being offered;

children.label: B
children.text: the fact that the post-transaction third party seller is not affiliated with the initial merchant, which may include disclosure of the name of the post-transaction third party in a manner that clearly differentiates the post-transaction third party seller from the initial merchant; and

children.label: C
children.text: the cost of such goods or services; and

children.label: i
children.text: the full account number of the account to be charged; and

children.label: ii
children.text: the consumer’s name and address and a means to contact the consumer; and

children.label: B
children.text: requiring the consumer to perform an additional affirmative action, such as clicking on a confirmation button or checking a box that indicates the consumer’s consent to be charged the amount disclosed.

children.heading: Prohibition on data-pass used to facilitate certain deceptive Internet sales transactions
children.label: b Prohibition on data-pass used to facilitate certain deceptive Internet sales transactions
children.text: It shall be unlawful for an initial merchant to disclose a credit card, debit card, bank account, or other financial account number, or to disclose other billing information that is used to charge a customer of the initial merchant, to any post-transaction third party seller for use in an Internet-based sale of any goods or services from that post-transaction third party seller.

children.heading: Application with other law
children.label: c Application with other law
children.text: Nothing in this chapter shall be construed to supersede, modify, or otherwise affect the requirements of the Electronic Funds Transfer Act ( et seq.) or any regulation promulgated thereunder.115 U.S.C. 1693So in original. Probably should be “Fund”.1

children.heading: Initial merchant
children.label: 1 Initial merchant
children.text: The term “initial merchant” means a person that has obtained a consumer’s billing information directly from the consumer through an Internet transaction initiated by the consumer.

children.label: A
children.text: sells, or offers for sale, any good or service on the Internet;

children.label: B
children.text: solicits the purchase of such goods or services on the Internet through an initial merchant after the consumer has initiated a transaction with the initial merchant; and

children.label: ii
children.text: a subsidiary or corporate affiliate of the initial merchant; or

children.label: iii
children.text: a successor of an entity described in clause (i) or (ii).
